eval

10 11 2020

Get and prepare raw data

library(readr)
data <- read_csv("data.csv")
## Rows: 492 Columns: 16
## ── Column specification ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
## Delimiter: ","
## chr  (7): subj_code, desktop_conf, attent_conf, device_mechanism, contingenc...
## dbl  (8): condition, structure_rating_prior, learning_yellow_correct_total, ...
## dttm (1): recorded_at
## 
## ℹ Use `spec()` to retrieve the full column specification for this data.
## ℹ Specify the column types or set `show_col_types = FALSE` to quiet this message.

Exclude participants whose learning accuracy (coded in “learning_yellow_accuracy” and “learning_pink_accuracy”) is less than 0.875

data_learning_exclusion <- data %>%
  filter(learning_yellow_accuracy < 7/8 | learning_pink_accuracy < 7/8)


data <- data %>%
  filter(learning_yellow_accuracy >= 7/8 & learning_pink_accuracy >= 7/8)
total_N <- nrow(data) + nrow(data_learning_exclusion)
# percentage excluded
excl_percentage <- (nrow(data_learning_exclusion) / total_N) * 100
excl_percentage
## [1] 26.53061
table(data$device_mechanism, data$contingency)
##         
##          0_0 05_05 1_1
##   opaque  60    60  60
##   transp  60    60  60
table(data$condition)
## 
##  1  2  3  4  5  6 
## 60 60 60 60 60 60

Demographics

# mean age of participants 
mean(data$age, na.rm = TRUE)
## [1] 42.26389
# age range of participants 
range(data$age, na.rm = TRUE)
## [1]  3 78

the one participant who indicated an age of 3 years wrote in the “tech_issues” field that they are actually 34 years old. So we will recode this participant’s age to 34.

data$age <- ifelse(data$age == 3, 34, data$age)

run the demographics code again

# mean age of participants 
mean(data$age, na.rm = TRUE)
## [1] 42.35
# age range of participants 
range(data$age, na.rm = TRUE)
## [1] 18 78
# number of genders reported 
table(data$gender)
## 
##              1: male            2: female        3: non-binary 
##                  187                  168                    3 
## 4: prefer not to say 
##                    2

Data preparation

Make factors

data$contingency <- factor(data$contingency, 
                              levels = c("1_1", "05_05", "0_0"), 
                              labels = c("P(e+|c+) 4/4 \nP(e+|c-) 4/4", "P(e+|c+) 2/4 \nP(e+|c-) 2/4", 
                                         "P(e+|c+) 0/4 \nP(e+|c-) 0/4"))


data$device_mechanism <- factor(data$device_mechanism, 
                              levels = c("opaque", "transp"), 
                              labels = c("opaque", "transparent"))

Analysis

Plots

Make a data frame in long format for plotting prior and posterior structure ratings together

# make a separate data frame in long format for plotting, with the DVs being "structure_rating_prior" and "strucutre_rating_posterior"
data_long <- data %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = c(structure_rating_prior, structure_rating_posterior),
               names_to = "causal_rating_type",
               values_to = "rating_value")

# make rating_type a factor with levels in the desired order
data_long$causal_rating_type <- factor(data_long$causal_rating_type, levels = c("structure_rating_prior", "structure_rating_posterior"),
                                labels = c("Prior", "Posterior"))
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(Hmisc)  # for mean_cl_boot
## 
## Attache Paket: 'Hmisc'
## Die folgenden Objekte sind maskiert von 'package:dplyr':
## 
##     src, summarize
## Die folgenden Objekte sind maskiert von 'package:base':
## 
##     format.pval, units
device_mechanism_colors <- c(
  # Dark2 Colors (Colors 1-8)
  "opaque"      = "#1b9e77",  # Dark2 Green
  "transparent"   = "#7570b3"  # Dark2 Purple
)



plotDV <- function(df, dv_col, x_jitter = 0.10, y_jitter = 5) {
  
  
  ggplot(df, aes(x = causal_rating_type, y = .data[[dv_col]], fill = device_mechanism)) +
    facet_grid(device_mechanism ~ contingency) +
    scale_y_continuous(limits = c(-10, 110), breaks = seq(0, 100, by = 10)) +
    
    # Reference line at y = 50
    geom_hline(yintercept = 50, color = "grey20", linewidth = 0.4, linetype = "dashed") +
    
    # Violin background (lighter, no outline)
    geom_violin(
      trim = TRUE, alpha = 0.25,
      color = NA
    ) +
    
    # Subject lines (jittered both horizontally & vertically)
    geom_line(
      aes(group = subj_code),
      alpha = 0.1,
      color = "grey50",
      position = position_jitter(width = x_jitter, height = y_jitter)
    ) +
    
    # Jittered points
    geom_point(
      alpha = 0.15,
      size = 1.2,
      color = "black",
      position = position_jitter(width = x_jitter, height = y_jitter)
    ) +
    
    # Bootstrapped mean ±95% CI ribbons (higher alpha)
    stat_summary(
      aes(group = device_mechanism, fill = device_mechanism),
      fun.data = mean_cl_boot,
      geom = "ribbon",
      alpha = 0.55,
      color = NA
    ) +
    
    # Mean line (per device_mechanism)
    stat_summary(
      aes(color = device_mechanism, group = device_mechanism),
      fun = mean,
      geom = "line",
      size = 1
    ) +
    
    # Mean point with thin black border
    stat_summary(
      aes(fill = device_mechanism),
      fun.data = mean_cl_boot,
      geom = "pointrange",
      shape = 21,
      color = "black",
      size = 0.9,
      stroke = 0.6
    ) +
    
    # Median point (white triangle)
    stat_summary(
      fun = median,
      geom = "point",
      shape = 24,
      size = 2.5,
      fill = "white",
      color = "black"
    ) +
    
    # Color scales
    scale_fill_manual(values = device_mechanism_colors) +
    scale_color_manual(values = device_mechanism_colors) +
    
    labs(
      x = "Structure Rating",
      y = "Rating Value"
    ) +
    
    # APA-friendly theme (no gridlines)
    theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
    theme(
      legend.position = "none",
      axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 25, hjust = 1),
      axis.text.y = element_text(size = 10),
      panel.spacing = unit(3, "lines"),
      panel.grid = element_blank(),
      strip.background = element_rect(fill = "white", color = "grey70", linewidth = 0.4),
      strip.text = element_text(face = "bold", size = 12),
      axis.title = element_text(face = "bold")
    )
}

# ---- Run and Save ----
p1 <- plotDV(data_long, "rating_value")
## Warning: Using `size` aesthetic for lines was deprecated in ggplot2 3.4.0.
## ℹ Please use `linewidth` instead.
## This warning is displayed once every 8 hours.
## Call `lifecycle::last_lifecycle_warnings()` to see where this warning was
## generated.
p1

ggsave("structure_rating_by_device_mechanism_APA_colored.svg", plot = p1, width = 8, height = 6)
ggsave("structure_rating_by_device_mechanism_APA_colored.pdf", plot = p1, width = 8, height = 6)

Statistical tests

Test the difference between prior and posterior structure ratings depending on device_mechanism and contingency condition using a mixed ANOVA with contingency and device_mechanism as between-subject factors and rating_type (prior vs. posterior) as within-subject factor

library(afex)
## Lade nötiges Paket: lme4
## Lade nötiges Paket: Matrix
## 
## Attache Paket: 'Matrix'
## Die folgenden Objekte sind maskiert von 'package:tidyr':
## 
##     expand, pack, unpack
## Das folgende Objekt ist maskiert 'package:reshape':
## 
##     expand
## ************
## Welcome to afex. For support visit: http://afex.singmann.science/
## - Functions for ANOVAs: aov_car(), aov_ez(), and aov_4()
## - Methods for calculating p-values with mixed(): 'S', 'KR', 'LRT', and 'PB'
## - 'afex_aov' and 'mixed' objects can be passed to emmeans() for follow-up tests
## - Get and set global package options with: afex_options()
## - Set sum-to-zero contrasts globally: set_sum_contrasts()
## - For example analyses see: browseVignettes("afex")
## ************
## 
## Attache Paket: 'afex'
## Das folgende Objekt ist maskiert 'package:lme4':
## 
##     lmer
library(emmeans)
## Welcome to emmeans.
## Caution: You lose important information if you filter this package's results.
## See '? untidy'
anova_data <- data_long

a1 <- aov_car(rating_value ~ device_mechanism*causal_rating_type*contingency  + Error(subj_code/causal_rating_type), anova_data, anova_table = list(es = "pes")) # the anova model
## Contrasts set to contr.sum for the following variables: device_mechanism, contingency
a1 
## Anova Table (Type 3 tests)
## 
## Response: rating_value
##                                            Effect     df     MSE          F
## 1                                device_mechanism 1, 354 1005.96 222.65 ***
## 2                                     contingency 2, 354 1005.96   7.44 ***
## 3                    device_mechanism:contingency 2, 354 1005.96     4.62 *
## 4                              causal_rating_type 1, 354  290.31     6.45 *
## 5             device_mechanism:causal_rating_type 1, 354  290.31  17.62 ***
## 6                  contingency:causal_rating_type 2, 354  290.31  16.15 ***
## 7 device_mechanism:contingency:causal_rating_type 2, 354  290.31   7.28 ***
##    pes p.value
## 1 .386   <.001
## 2 .040   <.001
## 3 .025    .011
## 4 .018    .012
## 5 .047   <.001
## 6 .084   <.001
## 7 .040   <.001
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '+' 0.1 ' ' 1

Repeat analysis as mixed effects model (same results in different format)

library(lme4)
library(lmerTest)  # gives p-values
## 
## Attache Paket: 'lmerTest'
## Das folgende Objekt ist maskiert 'package:lme4':
## 
##     lmer
## Das folgende Objekt ist maskiert 'package:stats':
## 
##     step
# drop the non-realized contingency levels 
anova_data <- droplevels(anova_data)
with(anova_data, table(device_mechanism, contingency))
##                 contingency
## device_mechanism P(e+|c+) 4/4 \nP(e+|c-) 4/4 P(e+|c+) 2/4 \nP(e+|c-) 2/4
##      opaque                              120                         120
##      transparent                         120                         120
##                 contingency
## device_mechanism P(e+|c+) 0/4 \nP(e+|c-) 0/4
##      opaque                              120
##      transparent                         120
model <- lmer(
  rating_value ~ device_mechanism * causal_rating_type * contingency +
    (1 | subj_code),
  data = anova_data
)

anova(model, type = 3)
## Type III Analysis of Variance Table with Satterthwaite's method
##                                                 Sum Sq Mean Sq NumDF DenDF
## device_mechanism                                 64639   64639     1   354
## causal_rating_type                                1872    1872     1   354
## contingency                                       4321    2161     2   354
## device_mechanism:causal_rating_type               5115    5115     1   354
## device_mechanism:contingency                      2680    1340     2   354
## causal_rating_type:contingency                    9377    4689     2   354
## device_mechanism:causal_rating_type:contingency   4229    2115     2   354
##                                                  F value    Pr(>F)    
## device_mechanism                                222.6523 < 2.2e-16 ***
## causal_rating_type                                6.4486 0.0115311 *  
## contingency                                       7.4426 0.0006820 ***
## device_mechanism:causal_rating_type              17.6178 3.419e-05 ***
## device_mechanism:contingency                      4.6151 0.0105048 *  
## causal_rating_type:contingency                   16.1502 1.940e-07 ***
## device_mechanism:causal_rating_type:contingency   7.2835 0.0007946 ***
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1

List of the group means

# means
group_means <- emmeans(a1, c("causal_rating_type", "contingency", "device_mechanism")) 
group_means
##  causal_rating_type  contingency                device_mechanism emmean   SE
##  Prior              P(e+|c+) 4/4 \nP(e+|c-) 4/4 opaque            58.27 3.03
##  Posterior          P(e+|c+) 4/4 \nP(e+|c-) 4/4 opaque            62.08 3.52
##  Prior              P(e+|c+) 2/4 \nP(e+|c-) 2/4 opaque            50.22 3.03
##  Posterior          P(e+|c+) 2/4 \nP(e+|c-) 2/4 opaque            45.63 3.52
##  Prior              P(e+|c+) 0/4 \nP(e+|c-) 0/4 opaque            53.67 3.03
##  Posterior          P(e+|c+) 0/4 \nP(e+|c-) 0/4 opaque            28.77 3.52
##  Prior              P(e+|c+) 4/4 \nP(e+|c-) 4/4 transparent       14.87 3.03
##  Posterior          P(e+|c+) 4/4 \nP(e+|c-) 4/4 transparent       18.95 3.52
##  Prior              P(e+|c+) 2/4 \nP(e+|c-) 2/4 transparent        9.45 3.03
##  Posterior          P(e+|c+) 2/4 \nP(e+|c-) 2/4 transparent       12.98 3.52
##  Prior              P(e+|c+) 0/4 \nP(e+|c-) 0/4 transparent       16.02 3.03
##  Posterior          P(e+|c+) 0/4 \nP(e+|c-) 0/4 transparent       14.72 3.52
##   df lower.CL upper.CL
##  354    52.30     64.2
##  354    55.16     69.0
##  354    44.25     56.2
##  354    38.71     52.6
##  354    47.70     59.6
##  354    21.84     35.7
##  354     8.90     20.8
##  354    12.02     25.9
##  354     3.48     15.4
##  354     6.06     19.9
##  354    10.05     22.0
##  354     7.79     21.6
## 
## Confidence level used: 0.95

compute a contrast with emmeans to test if the prior-posterior change is larger in the opaque device_mechanism condition compared to the transparent device_mechanism condition, across all contingency conditions

# main effect of device_mechanism
pairs(emmeans(a1, ~ device_mechanism), adjust = "none")
## NOTE: Results may be misleading due to involvement in interactions
##  contrast             estimate   SE  df t.ratio p.value
##  opaque - transparent     35.3 2.36 354  14.922  <.0001
## 
## Results are averaged over the levels of: contingency, causal_rating_type
# main effect of rating type
pairs(emmeans(a1, ~ causal_rating_type), adjust = "none")
##  contrast          estimate   SE  df t.ratio p.value
##  Prior - Posterior     3.23 1.27 354   2.539  0.0115
## 
## Results are averaged over the levels of: device_mechanism, contingency
#
pairs(emmeans(a1, ~ device_mechanism*causal_rating_type), adjust = "none")
##  contrast                                  estimate   SE  df t.ratio p.value
##  opaque Prior - transparent Prior             40.61 2.48 354  16.393  <.0001
##  opaque Prior - opaque Posterior               8.56 1.80 354   4.764  <.0001
##  opaque Prior - transparent Posterior         38.50 2.68 354  14.347  <.0001
##  transparent Prior - opaque Posterior        -32.05 2.68 354 -11.943  <.0001
##  transparent Prior - transparent Posterior    -2.11 1.80 354  -1.172  0.2418
##  opaque Posterior - transparent Posterior     29.94 2.88 354  10.414  <.0001
## 
## Results are averaged over the levels of: contingency
# rating differences across device_mechanism conditions
pairs(emmeans(a1, ~ causal_rating_type|device_mechanism), adjust = "none")
## device_mechanism = opaque:
##  contrast          estimate  SE  df t.ratio p.value
##  Prior - Posterior     8.56 1.8 354   4.764  <.0001
## 
## device_mechanism = transparent:
##  contrast          estimate  SE  df t.ratio p.value
##  Prior - Posterior    -2.11 1.8 354  -1.172  0.2418
## 
## Results are averaged over the levels of: contingency
# now test the difference in these deltas between the two device_mechanism conditions
pairs(pairs(emmeans(a1, ~ causal_rating_type*device_mechanism), adjust = "none"), adjust = "none")
##  contrast                                                                                
##  (Prior opaque - Posterior opaque) - (Prior opaque - Prior transparent)                  
##  (Prior opaque - Posterior opaque) - (Prior opaque - Posterior transparent)              
##  (Prior opaque - Posterior opaque) - (Posterior opaque - Prior transparent)              
##  (Prior opaque - Posterior opaque) - (Posterior opaque - Posterior transparent)          
##  (Prior opaque - Posterior opaque) - (Prior transparent - Posterior transparent)         
##  (Prior opaque - Prior transparent) - (Prior opaque - Posterior transparent)             
##  (Prior opaque - Prior transparent) - (Posterior opaque - Prior transparent)             
##  (Prior opaque - Prior transparent) - (Posterior opaque - Posterior transparent)         
##  (Prior opaque - Prior transparent) - (Prior transparent - Posterior transparent)        
##  (Prior opaque - Posterior transparent) - (Posterior opaque - Prior transparent)         
##  (Prior opaque - Posterior transparent) - (Posterior opaque - Posterior transparent)     
##  (Prior opaque - Posterior transparent) - (Prior transparent - Posterior transparent)    
##  (Posterior opaque - Prior transparent) - (Posterior opaque - Posterior transparent)     
##  (Posterior opaque - Prior transparent) - (Prior transparent - Posterior transparent)    
##  (Posterior opaque - Posterior transparent) - (Prior transparent - Posterior transparent)
##  estimate   SE  df t.ratio p.value
##    -32.05 2.68 354 -11.943  <.0001
##    -29.94 2.88 354 -10.414  <.0001
##    -23.49 3.84 354  -6.124  <.0001
##    -21.39 3.97 354  -5.384  <.0001
##     10.66 2.54 354   4.197  <.0001
##      2.11 1.80 354   1.172  0.2418
##      8.56 1.80 354   4.764  <.0001
##     10.66 2.54 354   4.197  <.0001
##     42.71 3.39 354  12.583  <.0001
##      6.45 2.54 354   2.539  0.0115
##      8.56 1.80 354   4.764  <.0001
##     40.61 2.48 354  16.393  <.0001
##      2.11 1.80 354   1.172  0.2418
##     34.16 3.55 354   9.627  <.0001
##     32.05 2.68 354  11.943  <.0001
## 
## Results are averaged over the levels of: contingency
pairs(emmeans(a1, ~ causal_rating_type|contingency*device_mechanism), adjust = "none") -> deltas

deltas
## contingency = P(e+|c+) 4/4 
## P(e+|c-) 4/4, device_mechanism = opaque:
##  contrast          estimate   SE  df t.ratio p.value
##  Prior - Posterior    -3.82 3.11 354  -1.227  0.2207
## 
## contingency = P(e+|c+) 2/4 
## P(e+|c-) 2/4, device_mechanism = opaque:
##  contrast          estimate   SE  df t.ratio p.value
##  Prior - Posterior     4.58 3.11 354   1.473  0.1415
## 
## contingency = P(e+|c+) 0/4 
## P(e+|c-) 0/4, device_mechanism = opaque:
##  contrast          estimate   SE  df t.ratio p.value
##  Prior - Posterior    24.90 3.11 354   8.004  <.0001
## 
## contingency = P(e+|c+) 4/4 
## P(e+|c-) 4/4, device_mechanism = transparent:
##  contrast          estimate   SE  df t.ratio p.value
##  Prior - Posterior    -4.08 3.11 354  -1.313  0.1902
## 
## contingency = P(e+|c+) 2/4 
## P(e+|c-) 2/4, device_mechanism = transparent:
##  contrast          estimate   SE  df t.ratio p.value
##  Prior - Posterior    -3.53 3.11 354  -1.136  0.2568
## 
## contingency = P(e+|c+) 0/4 
## P(e+|c-) 0/4, device_mechanism = transparent:
##  contrast          estimate   SE  df t.ratio p.value
##  Prior - Posterior     1.30 3.11 354   0.418  0.6763

Rating change categories

Compute the different between prior and posterior for each participant and append it to data frame

data <- data %>%
  mutate(rating_diff = structure_rating_posterior - structure_rating_prior)

Code for each participate what kind of rating difference occurred: no difference, increase, decrease. For the “no difference” type, allow for some noise of plus minus 5 points

data <- data %>%
  mutate(rating_diff_type = case_when(
    rating_diff > 0 ~ "increase",
    rating_diff < 0 ~ "decrease",
    TRUE ~ "same"
  ))

Now make a plot that shows the proportions of these rating_diff_type for each device_mechanism and contingency condition; as error bars, show 95% Proportion CIs (using binom.confint from the binom package)

library(binom)
data_diff_type <- data %>%
  group_by(device_mechanism, contingency, rating_diff_type) %>%
  summarise(
    count = n()
  ) %>%
  group_by(device_mechanism, contingency) %>%
  mutate(
    total = sum(count),
    proportion = count / total
  ) %>%
  ungroup()
## `summarise()` has grouped output by 'device_mechanism', 'contingency'. You can
## override using the `.groups` argument.
# compute 95% CIs for proportions
data_diff_type <- data_diff_type %>%
  rowwise() %>%
  mutate(
    ci = list(binom.confint(count, total, method = "wilson"))
  ) %>%
  unnest_wider(ci) %>%
  select(-c(method, mean, x))


ggplot(data_diff_type, aes(x = contingency, y = proportion, fill = rating_diff_type)) +
  facet_wrap(~ device_mechanism) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = position_dodge(width = 0.9), width = 0.7) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = lower, ymax = upper), 
                position = position_dodge(width = 0.9), width = 0.2) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("increase" = "#1b9e77", "same" = "#d95f02", "decrease" = "#7570b3")) +
  labs(
    title = "Proportion of Rating Change Types",
    x = "Contingency",
    y = "Proportion", 
    fill = "change type"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "top",
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 25, hjust = 1)
  )+ # define y axis limits so that they go from 0 to 1 in steps of 0.25
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 1), breaks = seq(0, 1, by = 0.25))-> p3
p3

# save plot 
ggsave("subgroups.svg", plot = p3, width = 14, height = 6)
ggsave("subgroups.pdf", plot = p3, width = 14, height = 6)

Determine the proportion of participants in the “transparent” condition who fell into the “same” cluster and a “rating_diff” of 0

data %>%
  filter(device_mechanism == "transparent" & rating_diff_type == "same") %>%
  summarise(
    count = n()
  ) -> transparent_same_count

data %>%
  filter(device_mechanism == "transparent" & rating_diff_type == "same" & structure_rating_prior == 0) %>%
  summarise(
    count = n()
  ) -> transparent_same_count_zero_prior


transparent_same_count_zero_prior/transparent_same_count
##       count
## 1 0.8181818

In the transparent conditions, of the participants who gave identical prior and posterior ratings, 82% had a prior rating of 0.

Generate Bayesian model predictions

# number of simulations 

m = 100000

SI model

# --- Load function file ---
source("SI_model_fun.R")

# --- Set up contingency tables ---
data <- matrix(data = 0, nrow = 5, ncol = 4)
#             D  C  B  A              
data[1,] <- c(0, 4, 0, 4) # 1.0 1.0
data[2,] <- c(1, 3, 1, 3) # 0.75 0.75
data[3,] <- c(2, 2, 2, 2) # 0.5 0.5
data[4,] <- c(3, 1, 3, 1) # 0.25 0.25
data[5,] <- c(4, 0, 4, 0) # 0.0 0.0

# --- Sweep over priors ---
prior_grid <- seq(0, 1, by = 0.1)

# Store all results in a list
all_preds <- list()

for (p0 in prior_grid) {
  p1 <- 1 - p0
  message("Running model with prior_S0 = ", p0, ", prior_S1 = ", p1)
  
  preds <- generate_SI_preds(
    data = data,
    m = m,
    prior_S0 = p0,
    prior_S1 = p1
  )
  
  # Add prior info as columns for later identification
  preds$prior_S0 <- p0
  preds$prior_S1 <- p1
  
  # Store results
  all_preds[[as.character(p0)]] <- preds
}
## Running model with prior_S0 = 0, prior_S1 = 1
## Running model with prior_S0 = 0.1, prior_S1 = 0.9
## Running model with prior_S0 = 0.2, prior_S1 = 0.8
## Running model with prior_S0 = 0.3, prior_S1 = 0.7
## Running model with prior_S0 = 0.4, prior_S1 = 0.6
## Running model with prior_S0 = 0.5, prior_S1 = 0.5
## Running model with prior_S0 = 0.6, prior_S1 = 0.4
## Running model with prior_S0 = 0.7, prior_S1 = 0.3
## Running model with prior_S0 = 0.8, prior_S1 = 0.2
## Running model with prior_S0 = 0.9, prior_S1 = 0.1
## Running model with prior_S0 = 1, prior_S1 = 0
# Combine all into one big data frame
library(dplyr)
pred_SI_all <- bind_rows(all_preds, .id = "prior_S0_label")

# make a subset containing columns 2:5, S0_pp, S1_pp, prior_S0, prior_S1
pred_SI_all <- pred_SI_all %>%
  select(N_00, N_01, N_10, N_11, S0_pp, S1_pp, prior_S0, prior_S1)


pred_SI_all$contingency <- pred_SI_all$N_11



pred_SI_all$contingency <- factor(pred_SI_all$contingency, 
                              levels = c("4", "3", "2", "1", "0"), 
                              labels = c("P(e+|c+) 4/4 \nP(e+|c-) 4/4", "P(e+|c+) 3/4 \nP(e+|c-) 3/4", "P(e+|c+) 2/4 \nP(e+|c-) 2/4", 
                                         "P(e+|c+) 1/4 \nP(e+|c-) 1/4", "P(e+|c+) 0/4 \nP(e+|c-) 0/4"))
library(ggplot2)

ggplot(pred_SI_all, aes(x = prior_S1, y = S1_pp, color = contingency, group = contingency)) +
  # 45° reference line
  geom_abline(slope = 1, intercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "gray40", linewidth = 1) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1.2) +
  geom_point(size = 2) +
  scale_x_continuous(
    name = expression("Prior: P(S"[1]*")"),
    breaks = seq(0, 1, 0.1),
    limits = c(0, 1)
  ) +
  scale_y_continuous(
    name = expression("Posterior: P(S"[1]*" | data)"),
    breaks = seq(0, 1, 0.1),
    limits = c(0, 1)
  ) +
  scale_color_viridis_d(option = "C", end = 0.9) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "right",
    legend.title = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    plot.title = element_text(size = 16, face = "bold", hjust = 0.5)
  ) +
  ggtitle(expression("Posterior belief in S1 as a function of prior belief in S1")) +
  coord_equal()+
  theme(
    plot.margin = margin(t = 0, r = 0, b = 0, l = 0)
  )

# save plot as svg 

ggsave("SI_model_prior_vs_posterior_S1_N8.svg", width = 7, height = 5)
ggsave("SI_model_prior_vs_posterior_S1_N8.pdf", width = 7, height = 5)
library(ggnewscale)
## Warning: Paket 'ggnewscale' wurde unter R Version 4.5.2 erstellt
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(grid)

# --- Prepare data ---
seg <- pred_SI_all %>%
  select(contingency, prior_S1, S1_pp) %>%
  transmute(
    contingency,
    id   = prior_S1,
    x0   = 0,   # Prior of S1
    x1   = 1,   # Posterior of S1
    y0   = prior_S1,
    y1   = S1_pp
  )

# Highlighted priors
seg_hi <- seg %>%
  filter(near(id, 0) | near(id, 0.5)) %>%
  mutate(highlight_label = ifelse(near(id, 0), "Prior = 0", "Prior = 0.5"))

# Endpoints for points + labels
endpts_hi <- seg_hi %>%
  tidyr::pivot_longer(c(x0, x1, y0, y1),
                      names_to = c(".value","which"),
                      names_pattern = "([xy])(\\d)") %>%
  mutate(
    which = ifelse(which == "0", "Prior of S1", "Posterior of S1"),
    x = ifelse(which == "Prior of S1", 0, 1),
    y = y
  )

# --- Plot ---
ggplot() +
  # Reference line at y = 50
    geom_hline(yintercept = 0.50, color = "grey20", linewidth = 0.4, linetype = "dashed") +
  # faint background arrows
  geom_segment(
    data = seg,
    aes(x = x0, xend = x1, y = y0, yend = y1),
    color = "black",
    linewidth = 0.8, alpha = 0.2,
    arrow = arrow(length = unit(0.15, "cm"), type = "closed")
  ) +

  # highlighted arrows (two distinct colors)
  geom_segment(
    data = seg_hi,
    aes(x = x0, xend = x1, y = y0, yend = y1, color = highlight_label),
    linewidth = 2.5, alpha = 0.85,
    arrow = arrow(length = unit(0.25, "cm"), type = "closed")
  ) +
  #geom_point(data = endpts_hi,aes(x = x, y = y, color = highlight_label),size = 3, alpha = 0.9) +
  geom_text(data = endpts_hi,
            aes(x = x, y = y, label = sprintf("%.2f", y)),
            vjust = -1.1, size = 3, color = "black") +

  facet_wrap(~contingency, nrow = 1) +
  scale_color_manual(
    name = "Highlighted priors",
    values = c(
      "Prior = 0"   = "#7570b3",  # red-orange #1b9e77", "transparent" = "#7570b3
      "Prior = 0.5" = "#1b9e77"   # teal
    ),
    guide = guide_legend(
      title.position = "top",
      override.aes = list(linewidth = 3, alpha = 1)
    )
  ) +
  scale_x_continuous(
    breaks = c(0, 1),
    labels = c(expression("Prior of S"[1]),
               expression("Post. of S"[1])),
    limits = c(-0.1, 1.1),
    name = NULL
  ) +
  scale_y_continuous(
    name = "Probability",
    limits = c(0, 1),
    breaks = seq(0, 1, 0.1)
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  theme(
  # …your other theme settings…
  panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
  panel.grid.minor.x = element_blank(),
  panel.grid.minor.y = element_blank(),
  panel.grid.major.y = element_blank(),
  panel.spacing.x = unit(1.5, "lines"),   # <-- NEW LINE: adds space between facets
  legend.position = "right",
  legend.title = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold"),
  legend.text = element_text(size = 10),
  strip.text = element_text(face = "bold", size = 12),
  plot.title = element_text(size = 16, face = "bold", hjust = 0.5),
  axis.text.x = element_text(size = 11,angle = 25, hjust = 1)
)+
  labs(
    title = expression("Prior–Posterior Updates for S"[1]*" across Contingency Conditions")
  )-> g_update_highlight

g_update_highlight

# Save plot
#ggsave("SI_model_prior_posterior_updates_highlight.svg", plot = g_update_highlight, width = 14, height = 5)
library(ggnewscale)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(grid)

# --- Subset contingencies of interest (8/8, 4/4, 0/0) ---
subset_data <- pred_SI_all %>%
  filter(N_11 %in% c(4, 2, 0))

# --- Prepare data ---
seg <- subset_data %>%
  select(contingency, prior_S1, S1_pp) %>%
  transmute(
    contingency,
    id   = prior_S1,
    x0   = 0,   # Prior of S1
    x1   = 1,   # Posterior of S1
    y0   = prior_S1,
    y1   = S1_pp
  )

# Highlighted priors
seg_hi <- seg %>%
  filter(near(id, 0) | near(id, 0.5)) %>%
  mutate(highlight_label = ifelse(near(id, 0), "Prior = 0", "Prior = 0.5"))

# Endpoints for points + labels
endpts_hi <- seg_hi %>%
  tidyr::pivot_longer(c(x0, x1, y0, y1),
                      names_to = c(".value","which"),
                      names_pattern = "([xy])(\\d)") %>%
  mutate(
    which = ifelse(which == "0", "Prior of S1", "Posterior of S1"),
    x = ifelse(which == "Prior of S1", 0, 1),
    y = y
  )

# --- Plot ---
ggplot() +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0.50, color = "grey20", linewidth = 0.4, linetype = "dashed") +
  geom_segment(
    data = seg,
    aes(x = x0, xend = x1, y = y0, yend = y1),
    color = "black",
    linewidth = 0.8, alpha = 0.2,
    arrow = arrow(length = unit(0.15, "cm"), type = "closed")
  ) +
  geom_segment(
    data = seg_hi,
    aes(x = x0, xend = x1, y = y0, yend = y1, color = highlight_label),
    linewidth = 2.5, alpha = 0.85,
    arrow = arrow(length = unit(0.25, "cm"), type = "closed")
  ) +
  geom_text(data = endpts_hi,
            aes(x = x, y = y, label = sprintf("%.2f", y)),
            vjust = -1.1, size = 3, color = "black") +
  facet_wrap(~contingency, nrow = 1) +
  scale_color_manual(
    name = "Highlighted priors",
    values = c(
      "Prior = 0"   = "#7570b3",
      "Prior = 0.5" = "#1b9e77"
    ),
    guide = guide_legend(
      title.position = "top",
      override.aes = list(linewidth = 3, alpha = 1)
    )
  ) +
  scale_x_continuous(
    breaks = c(0, 1),
    labels = c(expression("Prior of S"[1]),
               expression("Post. of S"[1])),
    limits = c(-0.1, 1.1),
    name = NULL
  ) +
  scale_y_continuous(
    name = "Probability",
    limits = c(0, 1),
    breaks = seq(0, 1, 0.1)
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  theme(
    panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor.x = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor.y = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.major.y = element_blank(),
    panel.spacing.x = unit(2, "lines"),
    legend.position = "right",
    legend.title = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold"),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 10),
    strip.text = element_text(face = "bold", size = 12),
    plot.title = element_text(size = 16, face = "bold", hjust = 0.5),
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = 11, angle = 0, hjust = 0.5)
  ) +
  labs(
    title = expression("Prior–Posterior Updates for S"[1]*"")
  ) -> g_update_highlight_subset

g_update_highlight_subset

# Save subset plot
ggsave("SI_model_prior_posterior_updates_highlight_subset_N8.svg",
       plot = g_update_highlight_subset, width = 10, height = 4)

ggsave("SI_model_prior_posterior_updates_highlight_subset_N8.pdf",
       plot = g_update_highlight_subset, width = 10, height = 4)
